raw:
{
  "judge_tag": "Judge-001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 3,
    "R1.6": 2,
    "RX.1": 2,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The output aligns evidence with claims but does not audit every claim against the table/figure.",
    "R1.6": "Improves a few problem paragraphs, but it's unclear if all cosmetic touches are present.",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty for one or two out-of-reach claims by mentioning preliminary nature of the efficiency claim and unverified numbers.",
    "RX.3": "Mostly within scope with minor drift as Twitter and Amazon improvements are mentioned but not detailed.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns by avoiding specificity in claims that cannot be verified.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, though some sources (BERT-base numbers) are not confirmed."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.8,
    "R1.6": 0.75,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.85,
    "RX.4": 0.95,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The output aligns evidence with claims but does not audit every claim against the table/figure.
rationale.R1.6: Improves a few problem paragraphs, but it's unclear if all cosmetic touches are present.
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for one or two out-of-reach claims by mentioning preliminary nature of the efficiency claim and unverified numbers.
rationale.RX.3: Mostly within scope with minor drift as Twitter and Amazon improvements are mentioned but not detailed.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns by avoiding specificity in claims that cannot be verified.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, though some sources (BERT-base numbers) are not confirmed.
